quality.rationale: 结合维里定理与四极矩辐射功率进行多步量纲分析，推导引力辐射反作用时标，逻辑链条完整且具有明确天体物理背景

origin.question: Problem 18.2. A gravitationally bound dynamical system (e.g. a binary star) has, in order of magnitude, mass M and size R. Estimate the time for radiation reaction forces to affect the system substantially, and compare this time-scale with the dynamical time-scale for the system.
origin.solution: Solution 18.2. The gravitational binding energy of the system is of order $\mathbf{M}^2 / \mathbf{R}$ and the kinetic energy is of order $\mathbf{MR}^2 / \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{dyn}}^2$ , where $\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{dyn}}$ characterizes motions of the system. For a system in equilibrium the virial theorem gives$$\mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}^{2} \sim \mathrm {R}^{3} / \mathrm {M}.$$The rate at which energy is radiated gravitationally is governed by the square of the third time derivative of the quadrupole moment or roughly$$\mathrm {P}^{\mathrm {G W}} \sim \mathrm {M}^{2} \mathrm {R}^{4} / \mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}^{6}.$$The time for radiation reaction to affect the system is the time it takes to radiate a significant fraction of its energy as gravitational waves:$$\mathrm {T} = \frac {\mathrm {K . E .}}{\mathrm {P}^{\mathrm {G W}}} \sim \frac {\mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}^{4}}{\mathrm {M R}^{2}} \sim \frac {\mathrm {R T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}^{2}}{\mathrm {M}^{2}}.$$The ratio of this gravitational wave characteristic time to the dynamical time is then of order$$\frac {\mathrm {T}}{\mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}} \sim \frac {\mathrm {R T}_{\mathrm {d y n}}}{\mathrm {M}^{2}} \sim \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {5}{2}}.$$Evidently gravitational radiation reaction forces play an appreciable role only in relativistically compact systems.
